note: "CRITICAL FINDING: A-09 0.1 m/s RMS threshold — ISO 2631-1 is occupational standard for able-bodied seated workers; Garcia-Mendez 2013 confirms wheelchair users are continuously exposed ABOVE ISO 2631-1 health caution zone in daily community use (0.83 ± 0.17 m/s2). Chenier & Aissaoui explicitly note ISO 2631-1 was not developed for SCI/disability populations. No disability-specific vibration standard exists for built environments. 0.1 m/s RMS cited in A-09 is NOT an ISO 2631-1 value — source of this specific threshold requires verification."

**Date:** 2026-03-18 23:59

**Pass type:** Targeted supplement — 9 core languages, Co-primary Tier 1 / Tier 2 scope only.

### Slugs Supplemented

| Slug | New sources added | Evidence gap confirmed |
|---|---|---|
| `mobility-wheelchair-built-environment\|MOB` | NHF Quality Criteria (NO), DHR 2025 (SV), APF/IFOP 2020 (FR), BSK e.V. (DE), DPI Japan 2023 (JA), CDPF supervision programme (ZH), Motionspot re-tiered to Co-primary Tier 1 | No new gaps |
| `visual-hearing-impairment-built-environment\|VIS\|DEAF` | RNIB Building Sight 2023 (EN), RNID (EN), Norges Blindeforbund re-tiered (NO) | RNIB Building Sight was critical gap — now resolved |
| `neurodivergent-mental-health-built-environment\|NDV\|MH` | Caldwell et al. 2025 (EN), SREF 2024 (EN), McAllister & McBeth 2022 (EN) | No new gaps |
| `neurological-dementia-built-environment\|NEU\|DEM` | Alzheimer Society re-tiered to Tier 2, BrainXchange Tier 2, Dementia Alliance International Co-primary Tier 1 | No new gaps |
| `chronic-pain-fatigue-spectrum-built-environment\|PAIN\|OFS` | PLRC (indirect), Bateman Horne Center (indirect) | CONFIRMED: no Co-primary Tier 1 or Tier 2 built environment guidance in any language — prior NO-DATA finding accurate |
| `deafblind-built-environment-design\|DBL` | Sense TouchBase Pears 2017 (Tier 2 practice example) | CONFIRMED: GAP-DBL-SEARCH-01 KNOWN-LIMITATION correct — zero guideline documents in any language |
| `intellectual-disability-built-environment-design\|IntD` | People First Canada Co-primary Tier 1, Inclusion International Tier 2 | THIN-BASE unchanged — no quantified design specifications from any source |

  - NO: No standard Norwegian built-environment term for signing space; TEK17 does not address DEAF spatial requirements; search via Norges Døveforbund advocacy materials
  - ZH: Signing space as a built-environment design category does not exist in Chinese standards (GB 50763-2012, GB 55019-2021); JFD equivalent (中国聋人协会) does not publish built-environment guidance; concept mapped from advocacy and academic literature only
  - JA: バリアフリー法 does not address Deaf spatial requirements beyond hearing loop provision; ろう学校 (Deaf school) design guidelines (MEXT) are the nearest relevant source; JFD (全日本ろうあ連盟) focuses on social welfare not built environment
  - KO: 편의증진법 addresses hearing loop provision only; 한국농아인협회 does not publish built-environment spatial guidance; field entirely nascent
  - DE: DIN 18040-1/2 does not address signing space; Deutscher Gehörlosen-Bund (DGB) guidance is for digital/media contexts; no German built-environment signing space standard exists; Bundesfachstelle Barrierefreiheit material is communication-access focused not spatial
  - IT: DM 236/89 and DPR 380/2001 do not address LIS/signing space; ENS Osservatorio Accessibilità focuses on communication barriers; Italian built-environment signing space guidance absent from standards
